I recomputed sha256 pins: analysis/a5.py 87f23a6905713eb439ca848e5da57585695cba97511de743f0a30d85abe7b349, kot_axiom.py d2064989c208c37df5a3d15a4fae6118c82516055fd6552f45a2a92fe2b7a659, kot_code.py 9fbe2a50dcd80e100a8a32ffb5d455e9ce993424fbf0bd293b8143aef96bb99a, gen_a5_corpora.py bd96e0d8324d23ed135aa830683429f0bc137a857a9788a558a2818e739612a7, a5_instrument.py fea02b6e4df24916e3488f05f94ed53e9992f6880e2ce161dd69e3ef6d78cbd7, and analysis-output.json aba98e8a830192d6d45c44d2bbca74050bb49eb45f9607437f312bd7704590ce. The kot-corpus-hash/1 digests matched all frozen corpus pins: a5-eval 3676d689277660f80f7cfac9823ecb7b9b40a73777ad538623405c5c1e903843, code-axioms-v0 dc930b4fdeb95994828d8b7b0a184e5d96714a3b7f51269f7d46cba5b14e39e3, code-corpus-v0 1063ad50ff694d8548296f42c17f296027768a87dba98f534e9f2ea7f7c5fea3, code-v0 01009b1fc0c6e34b0c49b294e0cb159695831070fbe6808957694f46d03abd83, code-world-v0 b8a8a50a3111425685cb2041061f72e4d0d89da17cb073fdffe11b338f26aef9, kernel-v0 8209cadabcfc2eaa11631c5c1100c04a48f33673516780b1f36cbf957217c809. The engine sha256 is byte-identical to the l3a harness pin, and pins_observed matched frozen pins in every eligible run. From the raw engine metrics, 855/855 covered exact over n=855 gives Wilson one-sided 95% LB 0.9968450437316213, and 122/122 strict control-refusal code matches over n=122 gives 0.9783007677455842; both match the verdict and analysis output float-for-float. Store violations are exactly 3 with one VIOLATION_DISJOINT, one VIOLATION_CARD_MAX, and one VIOLATION_RANGE. Baselines bracket the conjunction: abstain-all covered exact 0/855, refused-any 122/122, strict-code count 0; answer-all covered exact 855/855 and refused-any 0/122. Code review of kot_axiom.py, kot_code.py, and a5_instrument.py confirmed deterministic sorted indexes, no randomness, no heuristic/similarity/fallback guessing in the engine, no expected-field reads by the engine or query layer, expected consumed only in the instrument scorer, fail-closed named ERR_* refusals, answers carrying provenance plus non-empty license, and scorer validation that provenance ids are in the world store. The X3 binding check found exact sourceId-to-URN lookup in gen_a5_corpora.py and kot_code.py via minted-urns.jsonl; axiom/world/eval valid concept URNs resolve to minted code-v0 or kernel-v0 entries with matching sourceIds, with the sole non-minted urn:kot:UPPER occurring inside a malformed control query whose expected refusal is ERR_BAD_QUERY. I found no nearest-neighbour, embedding, similarity, or fuzzy matching path. Generator separation holds: gen_a5_corpora.py does not import kot_axiom.py or kot_code.py and computes expectations from extraction tables. For leakage, I independently built class and relation indexes directly from data/code-world-v0/world.jsonl and sampled two queries from every family, 38 samples across 9 covered and 10 control families; all derived values or ERR_* codes matched data/a5-eval. I also spot-checked extracted source facts against data/code-corpus-v0/src, including claims-check call edges, l3a_instrument imports, and test_fixtures class/defines records. The 10 planted world records are provenance-flagged as planted-violation/1 and independently recompute to exactly the three planted violations. Applying frozen verdict rules top-down over analysis-output gives rule 0 INSTRUMENT-INVALID false, rule 1 FAIL false, rule 2 PASS true. I found no confirmed non-runner a5 audit record on disk; with runner-3 as runner and not auditor-2, PASS-PENDING-AUDIT is the correct G-6 state. Scope text in the frozen record and verdict explicitly limits the claim to the HA5 engine leg over the closed code grammar and excludes engine-vs-LLM, NL, RAG, cost-ratio, other-codebase/language, extraction-soundness-vs-runtime, and kernel-usefulness claims; endpoints and rules do not broaden that scope."}
